titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Donald Whittaker collection relating to 20th century culture
titlestmt.author: by Emily Komornik

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.3830463

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday June 2, 2022 at 03:50

did.unittitle: Donald Whittaker collection relating to 20th century culture

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Donald Whittaker collection relating to 20th century culture contains photographs, stereographs, printed materials, and other papers documenting late 19th and 20th century culture. The collection includes photographs of art, architecture, and landscapes in locations across Europe and Africa, headshots of actors, film stills, nude and erotic photographs, and erotic stereographs. Also included are printed materials, such as books in German and Italian, and broadsides advertising American and Spanish films.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Visual Materials, 1873-1993 and undated. II. Printed Textual Materials, 1926-1968 and undated.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Donald Whittaker Collection relating to 20th Century Culture. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Donald Whittaker collection relating to 20th century culture contains photographs, stereographs, printed materials, and other papers documenting late 19th and 20th century culture. The collection includes photographs of art, architecture, and landscapes in locations across Europe and Africa, headshots of actors, film stills, nude and erotic photographs, and erotic stereographs. Also included are printed materials, such as books in German and Italian, and broadsides advertising American and Spanish films.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Donald Whittaker collection relating to 20th century culture is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes photographs showcasing art, architecture, and landscapes from locations across Europe and Africa, such as Algeria, Austria, Belgium, France, Italy, and Switzerland. Printed postcards feature artwork from the Museum of Luxembourg, the Louvre, and the National Museum of Italy at Napoli. Also included are art study photographs, film stills, and files from Soho Weekly, an alternative newspaper published in New York City from 1973 to 1982. Soho Weekly files include headshots of actors and performers such as Chubby Checker, Ray Charles, Elizabeth Taylor, Shura Cherkassky, and Lydia Clarke, as well as photographs of casts and crews from the productions Wedding Banquet, A Very Natural Thing, Visions of Light, and Via Appia. Also included in this series are erotic photographs and stereographs.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under four headings: Art, Architecture, and Views; Postcards; Nudes and Erotic Images; and 20th Century Culture. Materials maintain the order found at the time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Film stills, Man with the Golden Arm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of three books in German and Italian, and three broadsides advertising American and Spanish films.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under two headings: Books, and Broadsides. Arranged alphabetically by folder title.

did.unittitle: Der Fliegende Mensch: Gymnastiche Sprungübungen , August Glucker
Le Muse Svestite , Tano Citeroni and Alberto Manodori
Von Leibeszucht und Leibesschönheit , Kurt Reichert
